resources.string: Control your spending in a simple, educational, and efficient way with a method that adapts to your life.
Define monthly goals for your spending (like housing, leisure, or groceries). Your planning is the guide so you never spend more than you should.
Your balance only updates when money actually comes in. Record your real earnings to see your monthly balance grow.
Keep an eye on the icons (ℹ️). They explain each part of the app and bring valuable financial education tips for your daily life.
Enter the total amount you plan to receive this month. We will use this value as a reference to define your spending goals.
TIP: You can redefine this value whenever you want in the settings. Your real monthly balance only updates when you record your earnings as \'Income\' in \'New Transaction\'.
How do you want to divide your plan?
Choose how to distribute your planning among the financial pillars. You can use a ready-made method or create your own.
Vital expenses for rent, mortgage, utilities, and internet.
Fuel, public transportation, or vehicle maintenance.
Pharmacy, health plans, or medical appointments.
Courses, books, or school tuition fees.
Expenses that improve your quality of life (e.g., gym, grooming).
Clothing, electronics, and other non-essential items.
Netflix, Spotify, apps and recurring services.
Money set aside for unforeseen events and immediate security.
Funds allocated to generate passive income in the long term.
Targeted savings for specific dreams (e.g., travel, car).
Amount must be greater than zero
Focus on balance: 50% for basics, 30% for enjoying today, and 20% for your future.
Focus on freedom: Keeps essentials at 50% and increases investment to 25% to speed up your independence.
The financial goals below are based on the value defined in your Monthly Planning. You can adjust this value in the Settings at any time.
Vital expenses for survival (Housing, Food, Health). The limit shown is based on your Monthly Planning.\n\nTIP: If this group exceeds 50%, you might be living above your current means.

Expenses for comfort and pleasure, but not vital (Leisure, Subscriptions, Hobbies). The limit shown is based on your Monthly Planning.\n\nTIP: This is the first place to cut costs if you need to save.

Money destined for your future and security (Emergency Fund, Investments). The limit shown is based on your Monthly Planning.\n\nTIP: Treat this amount as a mandatory bill. Pay yourself first as soon as you receive your income.
Avoid the trap of increasing your cost of living whenever your income increases.
Available balance reflects your real money today (Income - Expenses). To update it, record your earnings (salary, extra income, etc.) as \'Income\' in \'New Transaction\'.
TIP: Readjusting this value automatically recalculates all your spending goals. Your real available balance continues to be updated through \'New Transaction\'.
This category has transactions linked to it. If you delete it, these transactions will be uncategorized and might be lost in history. Are you sure?
